title: Add --max-workers and heuristics for parallel operations
body:
add a global flag for --max-workers so users can limit the number
of parallel operations for a given function.  also, when not limited
by max-workers, we implement a heuristic function that returns the
number of preferred parallel workers based on the number of CPUs and
the given operation.

Signed-off-by: baude <bbaude@redhat.com>

comments.body: @baude looks like some of the rootless tests aren't hip.
Who do you envision every using this option.

I'm the troublemaker on this one.  My thinking was if the default setting ever caused performance issues, or we found that the machine had room to let more crank through then the default and it helped speed up processing, it would be good to have the toggle available.

Given that, I don't anticipate many folks will use it, but I'd bet my bottom dollar we'll land in a situation where it will be useful some day.

@rhatdan @mheon and @TomSweeneyRedHat made me promise to implement this option so I cannot speak to that.  That said, I could see where someone might want to eliminate what they might consider to be too much parallelism
Someone is going to need this (esp. in memory-constrained situations) to not blow up their system with a thousand parallel runc processes
Is this a podman thing or a libpod thing?  If a libpod thing, I would prefer it to go into libpod.conf.
Probably a Podman/Varlink thing more than a Libpod thing - I don't think we want this in libpod, it doesn't strike me as desirable in CRI-O

Well I can hold my nose on this.  I am not a big fan of adding options, that just clutter up the CLI and no one ever uses.  Plus we need to tests what happens when I set the number to 0?  What happens when I set it to 100,000?  Does this options cause breakage on different kinds of systems?

We should detect 0 and throw angry errors. 10k... I don't actually know if we ALWAYS spin up the maximum number of workers, or if we only spin then up proportionate to the number of containers. It's probably safe if it's the number of containers, we'll just end up with gigabytes of memory spent on runc.
@rhatdan when you are done with your spiel, lets talk it out ... i'm flexible here.  would you prefer I leave the --max-workers part out for now ?
I think were fixing a theoretical problem.
made compromise with @rhatdan ... please /lgtm when green
